_comment: Example AIP Step Execution Token. A Step Execution Token is a Registry-issued JWT whose payload includes aip_approval_id, aip_step_kind, and either aip_approval_step or aip_compensation_step. It is issued to the step actor when the actor claims a claimable step via POST /v1/approvals/{approval_id}/steps/{step_index}/claim. The Relying Party validates it using the SET validation profile, then Step 10a approval step verification. Signatures are placeholders.

aip_chain: <compact JWT for Principal Token at delegation_depth=0 — see delegation-chain.json>

_notes.ttl: exp - iat = 300 seconds in this example. The effective TTL cap is the lowest ttl_max_seconds value across requested scopes in the synced AIP Scope Catalog; the example assumes transactions has ttl_max_seconds=300. DPoP is REQUIRED for Tier 2 operations.
_notes.issuer: iss is the Registry ID, not the actor AID. The header aip_trv selects the locally pinned Registry Trust Record version, and kid is listed under active_verification_keys.step_execution in that version.
_notes.aip_approval_id_and_step: aip_approval_id and aip_step_kind are REQUIRED in every Step Execution Token. For forward steps, aip_approval_step is REQUIRED and aip_compensation_step MUST be absent. For compensation steps, aip_compensation_step is REQUIRED and aip_approval_step MUST be absent.
_notes.aip_approval_step_1based: aip_approval_step=2 is 1-based — it identifies step_index=2 in the Approval Envelope. The Relying Party MUST verify this equals the step it is executing. The path parameter {n} in GET /v1/approvals/{id}/steps/{n} MUST equal this value verbatim.
_notes.relying_party_validation: The Relying Party (payments.bank.example.com) validates: (1) SET validation profile, (2) Step 10a: verify aip_approval_id envelope is approved/executing, step 2 is claimed (not already completed), and action_hash matches the expected action, (3) DPoP proof required for transactions scope and bound to the actor AID in sub.
_notes.step_claim_flow: The service agent (payment-processor) calls POST /v1/approvals/apr:7e3b4c0d-f5a6-4b8e-9c1d-2e3f4a5b6c7d/steps/2/claim. The Registry checks: envelope is approved/executing, step 2 triggered_by=1 is completed, step 2 is pending. If valid, Registry issues this token (status→claimed, claimed_at set) and the agent presents it to payments.bank.example.com.
_notes.engagement_scope: aip_engagement_id links this token to the procurement engagement. The Relying Party MUST verify the engagement is active during Step 6b engagement validation.
_notes.actor_binding: The actor AID is sub. The SET validation profile requires aip_chain[n-1].sub to equal this value.
